← all tutorials

Network Partitions & Split-Brain — interactive 📖 NETWORK_PARTITIONS.md 📄 network_partitions.py

📖 Full guide  ·  📄 Ground-truth source — every panel below recomputes the same model in JS.
A wall drops between the nodes: {A,B} can't hear {C,D,E}. Click a link to cut it (or use the presets). Only a side reaching quorum = 3 can elect a leader. Toggle CP vs AP to see how each handles the same write. Then watch the split-brain conflict get resolved and healed. All numbers are recomputed in JS from the identical model as network_partitions.py.

① The topology — 5 nodes, K₅. Cut links to make a partition; quorum decides the leader

What to do: click any link to cut/restore it, or use the buttons. The page recomputes connected components live and asks: which side has ≥ quorum (3)? That side elects a leader; the other is stuck.
leader can't lead (no quorum) cut link split-brain risk
Quorum = floor(N/2)+1 = floor(5/2)+1 = 3. Two disjoint sides sum to 5 < 2·3, so they can never both reach quorum — that asymmetry is exactly what makes split-brain impossible under quorum-based consensus.

② CAP under partition — Consistency (CP) vs Availability (AP)

What this shows: a client in each side writes balance during the {A,B}|{C,D,E} partition. CP rejects the minority write (no leader → stays consistent); AP accepts both (available → diverges). Toggle and compare.

③ Split-brain — two leaders, two conflicting writes (vector-clock check, live)

The disaster: safety breaks — both sides elect a leader and write the SAME key. The page recomputes the happens-before / concurrent test on the two vector clocks. concurrent = a real conflict, not a stale read.

④ Resolving the conflict — LWW vs vector clocks vs CRDT (same two writes)

Three strategies on the same conflict (balance 100 vs 200, concurrent). Pick one. Toggle clock skew to see LWW silently lose data; watch the G-Counter CRDT merge with no conflict.

⑤ Healing — the partition ends; CP resyncs, AP merges

Convergence: CP minority discards its (empty) stale log and copies the leader (0 conflicts); AP runs anti-entropy (Merkle/gossip) + read repair and merges (no writes lost).
CP healing (etcd / ZooKeeper)
AP healing (Cassandra / Dynamo)
check: … JS recompute matches network_partitions.py: quorum=3, leader=C, w1⊥w2 concurrent, LWW=200 (skew=100), CRDT merge=5, CP final all=200.
